standard: CEQ NEPA and Permitting Data and Technology Standard v1.2 (August 18, 2025)
mfr: MFR 4 — GIS layer inventory; MFR 6 — Five external GIS services for proximity screening
description: Registry of the GIS services in the NEPA_GIS_Layer__mdt catalog. Each layer is configured for dynamic proximity checking using the project's latitude/longitude coordinates. Results are written to nepa_gis_data_element__c records and aggregate flags are set on the IndividualApplication. NOTE: The OmniStudio Integration Procedure invocation path (NEPA_GISProximityCheck) is BACKLOG — not verified end-to-end. The GIS layer catalog and schema are delivered; the end-to-end callout path has not been confirmed. See OMNISTUDIO-BACKLOG.md.

integration_architecture.mechanism: OmniStudio Integration Procedure — NEPA_GISProximityCheck (BACKLOG — not verified). Working trigger: NEPA_GIS_Proximity_Check Flow + NepaGISProximityIPInvoker Apex bridge.
integration_architecture.invocation: Apex bridge class NepaGISProximityIPInvoker.invokeProcedure() called from NEPA_Stage_Gate flow on intake submission
output_objects: nepa_gis_data_element__c (one record per layer per run)
output_fields_on_application: nepa_extraordinary_circumstances__c — boolean set true when any layer triggers EC keyword
nepa_gis_last_checked__c — timestamp of last proximity check run
nepa_nhd_proximity_flag__c — true when NHD waterway intersects project buffer (MFR 6)
nepa_tribal_lands_flag__c — true when BLM tribal boundary intersects project buffer (MFR 6)
nepa_plss_flag__c — true when BLM PLSS public land survey system intersects project buffer (MFR 6)
integration_architecture.ce_screener_integration: NEPA_CE_Screener flow reads nepa_nhd_proximity_flag__c, nepa_tribal_lands_flag__c, nepa_plss_flag__c and routes to set nepa_extraordinary_circumstances__c = true when any flag is active

layers.data_source: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service Environmental Conservation Online System (ECOS)
layers.regulatory_basis: ESA Section 7 — federal actions may not jeopardize listed species or destroy/adversely modify critical habitat (16 U.S.C. § 1536(a)(2))
layers.nepa_relevance: Critical habitat designation within project buffer is an extraordinary circumstance under 40 C.F.R. § 1501.4(b). Triggers ESA Section 7 consultation requirement and CE disqualifier.

layers.data_source: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Environmental Justice Screening and Mapping Tool (EJScreen)
layers.api_standard: ArcGIS REST Feature Service / EPA GeoPub REST API
layers.regulatory_basis: EO 12898 Environmental Justice; CEQ Environmental Justice Guidance (January 2023); OMB M-24-10 Section 4.3 — EJ communities require heightened comment processing
layers.nepa_relevance: Projects in census tracts with EJScreen Index > 80th percentile require EJ-specific alternatives analysis and enhanced public engagement. Triggers NEPA_EJTribal_Router for comment processing.

layers.data_source: U.S. Geological Survey National Hydrography Dataset (NHD) — National Map
layers.regulatory_basis: Clean Water Act Section 404 — dredge and fill of waters of the US requires Army Corps of Engineers Section 404 permit; Section 401 water quality certification required (33 U.S.C. § 1344). NEPA extraordinary circumstances: 40 C.F.R. § 1501.4(b)(vii)
layers.nepa_relevance: Waterway within 0.5-mile buffer triggers CWA Section 404/401 permitting requirement. Sets nepa_nhd_proximity_flag__c = true on IndividualApplication; NEPA_CE_Screener routes to set nepa_extraordinary_circumstances__c = true and requires additional permitting documentation.

layers.data_source: Bureau of Land Management — National Integrated Land System (NILS); Tribal Boundaries layer
layers.regulatory_basis: NHPA Section 106 — federal undertakings must consult with tribes on historic properties including sacred sites (54 U.S.C. § 306108). EO 13175 — consultation and coordination with tribal governments. NEPA extraordinary circumstances: CEQ 2022 regulations § 1501.4(b)(vi)
layers.nepa_relevance: Project intersection with tribal or trust lands triggers mandatory government-to-government consultation under EO 13175 and NHPA Section 106. Sets nepa_tribal_lands_flag__c = true on IndividualApplication; NEPA_CE_Screener routes to set nepa_extraordinary_circumstances__c = true. Comment processing routes EJ/tribal comments via NEPA_EJTribal_Router.
layers.plaintiff_intelligence: Tribal Nation challengers achieve 87.5% win rate in PermitTEC corpus — highest of any plaintiff category. Tribal lands flag is a strong litigation risk predictor.

layers.data_source: Bureau of Land Management — National Integrated Land System (NILS); Public Land Survey System (PLSS) Townships and Ranges
layers.regulatory_basis: Federal Land Policy and Management Act (FLPMA) — BLM surface estate management requires NEPA review for authorizations on federal public land (43 U.S.C. § 1732). Right-of-way grants on PLSS parcels require surface use plan and bonding.
layers.nepa_relevance: Project footprint on BLM public land (PLSS) confirms federal nexus and BLM surface management authority. Sets nepa_plss_flag__c = true on IndividualApplication; informs agency routing and required permit matrix. Supports documentation that BLM is the lead agency for CE or EA/EIS determination.

configuration_management.description: GIS layer endpoints and parameters are stored as NEPA_GIS_Layer__mdt custom metadata records (delivered), not hardcoded in the Integration Procedure. This allows endpoint updates without code deployment. The Integration Procedure itself is backlog — not verified end-to-end.
key_fields: Named_Credential__c — Salesforce Named Credential API name
Extraordinary_Circumstances_Keyword__c — keyword to match in layer response for EC flag
Result_Key_Field__c — response field to extract as feature name
